title: Boat Rental in Datca: Prices, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Chartering a boat in Datca opens up the turquoise waters of the Aegean Sea, with daily hire from a private gulet at around 250 EUR to a fully crewed yacht at 1,500 EUR. For a reliable platform to compare options, use boat hire Datca on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Datca or list your own.

Where to set off in Datca

Most boat rentals depart from Datca Marina or the town harbour, with easy access to nearby bays and islands. Datca Marina is the main hub, offering berths and charter offices. Alternatively, you can launch from Kargi Bay or Palamutbuku, both sheltered coves ideal for family trips. Further afield, the island of Symi is a popular day destination, about 12 nautical miles west. For a quieter start, try Ovabuku or Hayitbuku, each with small jetties and clear water. You can boat hire Datca for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

Choose your boat based on group size, experience and budget, from 150 EUR for a day out.

  1. Motorboat (day cruiser), best for couples or small families exploring nearby coves; up to 6 guests; from 200 EUR half-day. For a well-equipped example, see the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing.
  2. Gulet, traditional wooden yacht for a relaxed day or week; up to 12 guests; from 600 EUR full-day.
  3. Sailing yacht, ideal for those who enjoy wind-powered cruising; up to 8 guests; from 350 EUR full-day.
  4. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), fast and practical for water sports or fishing; up to 6 guests; from 150 EUR half-day.
  5. Crewed luxury motor yacht, for a premium experience with captain and host; up to 10 guests; from 1,200 EUR full-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Datca?

Prices range from 40 EUR per person for a shared tour to 1,500 EUR per day for a crewed yacht.

  • Shared boat tour: 40, 80 EUR per person, usually 4 hours including snorkelling gear.
  • Small motorboat half-day: 150, 250 EUR for up to 6 people, no licence needed for models under 15 hp.
  • Catamaran full-day: 700, 1,200 EUR for up to 12 guests, with skipper and fuel included.
  • Gulet weekly charter: 3,000, 7,000 EUR for 7 nights, all-inclusive with crew.
  • Crewed luxury yacht: 1,000, 1,500 EUR per day for up to 10 guests, with captain and host.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Datca?

If the boat has an engine over 15 hp you need a valid boating licence; the operator confirms exact requirements for each booking. For smaller powered boats or sailing vessels under 15 metres, no licence is typically required, but you must be over 18 and demonstrate basic handling ability.

In high season (July to August), the meltemi wind can pick up in the afternoon, so start your day before 10 am to enjoy calm seas and secure the best anchorage at sites like Palamutbuku.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Datca?

The ideal months are May to October, with peak season in July and August offering the warmest water and reliable meltemi breezes. May and June are quieter with temperatures around 25°C, while September and October still provide swimming conditions but lower prices and fewer crowds. In 2026, book well in advance for July and August charters.

At a glance

  • Price range: 150, 1,500 EUR per day
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Trip length: half-day to full-week
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: required for engines over 15 hp

Questions about boat rental in Datca

Can I rent a boat in Datca without a licence?

Yes, for small motorboats with up to 15 hp or sailing yachts without an engine, no licence is required; the operator will confirm on booking.

What is the best anchorage near Datca for swimming?

Palamutbuku offers sheltered turquoise water and a sandy beach, ideal for a swim stop and lunch afloat.

How long does it take to sail from Datca to Symi?

The crossing is roughly 12 nautical miles and takes about 1 hour by motorboat or 2, 3 hours by sail, depending on wind.

What currency is used for boat hire in Datca?

Prices are usually quoted in EUR, but many local operators also accept Turkish lira; confirm at the time of booking.

Whether you choose a half-day motorboat or a crewed gulet, BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners in Datca. Boat owners can also list your boat on the platform to reach travellers planning their 2026 Aegean holiday.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Datca and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
